text:
Once upon a time there was a young man named Tom who visited the library every weekend. Tom loved to discover new books. One peculiar book called "The Whispering Winds" intrigued him. Every Saturday afternoon, Tom would curl up with that book.

One sunny afternoon, Tom forgot his sunglasses. When he came across a small park around the library, he decided to take a break and admire the colorful butterflies fluttering around. That's when he heard a gentle voice.

"Could you teach me about butterflies?"

Tom looked around, wondering who could speak to him so casually. Walking closer, he met a wise-looking woman, her skin fair and her eyes twinkling with kindness.

"Yes, dear. What an interesting question! There are many kinds of butterflies. Have you ever seen a peacock feather? That's a brightly colored feather worn only by birds that specialize in shimmering colors."

Tom nodded. "That sounds interesting!"
